title: NOC 72200 – Electricians (except industrial and power system) | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72200 professionals are the essential technical force behind Canada’s residential and commercial infrastructure. As a TEER 2 occupation, these electricians lay out, assemble, install, test, troubleshoot, and repair electrical wiring, fixtures, and control devices. In 2026, as Canada mandates "Net-Zero" building standards and the rapid expansion of EV Charging Networks, electricians have been re-classified as "High-Priority Green Talent." For immigration, this role is a premier candidate for Express Entry Category-Based Selection and the Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group722 – Electrical trades and electrical power line and cable workersMinor Group7220 – Electricians (except industrial and power system)NOC Code72200

duties: Layout & Installation: Read and interpret drawings, circuit diagrams, and electrical code specifications to determine wiring layouts for new or existing installations.
Wiring: Pull wire through conduits and through holes in walls and floors; install brackets and hangers to support electrical equipment.
Connectivity: Install, replace, and repair lighting fixtures and electrical control and distribution equipment, such as switches, relays, and circuit breaker panels.
Troubleshooting: Test electrical and electronic equipment and components for continuity, current, voltage, and resistance using multimeters and other testers.
Safety & Compliance: Ensure all installations meet the Canadian Electrical Code (CEC); identify and isolate faults in systems to prevent fire or shock hazards.
Smart Technology: Install data cabling, fiber optics, and automated building control systems.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 4-to-5-year apprenticeship program is the standard route.

Work Experience: Alternatively, a combination of over 5 years of work experience in the trade and some high school, college, or industry courses in electricity may be accepted for trade certification.

Trade Certification (Mandatory): * Trade certification is compulsory in Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Saskatchewan, and Alberta.It is available, but voluntary, in British Columbia, Manitoba, and the territories.
It is available, but voluntary, in British Columbia, Manitoba, and the territories.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for all immigrants to ensure national recognition and maximum PR points.
salary_prospects: Note: Fully licensed Journeypersons in major urban centers often earn annual salaries between $85,000 and $115,000 inclusive of overtime and specialized service call premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that nearly 25% of the current electrical workforce will retire by 2030. Combined with the federal mandate to switch all new light-duty vehicles to zero-emission by 2035, the demand for electricians to build the supporting infrastructure ensures decades of job security.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Electricians in 2026 is Very Good to Critical. In Ontario, British Columbia, and Nova Scotia, a massive backlog in housing construction and the "Federal Retrofit Program" have created a severe shortage of journeypersons. According to BuildForce Canada 2026 projections, the industry requires over 12,000 new electricians by 2028 to replace retiring workers and support the electrification of the national transport grid.
The "Electric Age" Lead: Be at the forefront of the green transition by installing smart-home systems, solar arrays, and high-speed EV charging infrastructure.
Red Seal Interprovincial Mobility: With a Red Seal, you can work anywhere in Canada, from the high-rises of Toronto to the tech hubs of Vancouver.
Entrepreneurial Opportunities: This trade has one of the highest rates of successful self-employment and small-business ownership in Canada.
Direct PR Pathway: Electricians are a permanent fixture in Express Entry "Trade" category draws, often receiving invitations with significantly lower CRS scores than general applicants.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary route for electricians with at least 2 years of work experience and a job offer or certificate of qualification.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Skilled Worker Stream): A high-priority route for electricians moving to Ontario’s construction sector.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure for skilled workers already in Canada contributing to the "National Housing Strategy."
